info.title: Dexploit API
info.description: Real-time and historical Solana DEX swap and OHLCV data.

BearerAuth.description: Required by the OHLCV endpoints (`/api/v1/*`) and accepted by every other endpoint. Send `Authorization: Bearer ohlcv_live_sk_<your_key>`.

ApiKeyQuery.name: api_key
ApiKeyQuery.description: Browser-friendly alternative to the Bearer header — accepted by every endpoint and required for WebSocket from the browser.

message.example: use pair_address; see /api/v1/pairs?token_address=X to discover pools

recent_volume_sol.description: Volume in SOL over a recent rolling window

base_mint.description: Base side of the pool — almost always wrapped SOL.

volume_sol.description: **Lamports**, despite the field name. Divide by 1e9 to get SOL.

volume_token.description: Token base units. Divide by 10**`quote_decimals` for human-readable.

DexId.description:
Integer DEX identifier used in raw swap rows. Mapping:

| ID | DEX |
| -- | --- |
| 1  | `pumpfun` |
| 2  | `pumpswap` |
| 3  | `raydium_amm` |
| 4  | `raydium_clmm` |
| 5  | `raydium_cpmm` |
| 6  | `orca` |
| 7  | `meteora_damm_v2` |
| 8  | `meteora_dbc` |
| 9  | `meteora_dlmm` |
| 10 | `meteora_pools` |

Swap.description:
A single swap event from the raw swaps index. Same shape across `/swaps`, `/swaps/range`, `/swaps/whale`, `/swaps/{signature}`, `/swaps/trader/{wallet}`, `/swaps/token/{mint}`.

Note that the streaming feeds (WebSocket and gRPC) use a *different* shape — see [WebSocket wire format](/streaming/websocket) — most notably `swap_type` (string) vs `is_buy` (bool) here, and `dex` as the string name vs `dex` as the integer ID here.

token_address.description: Non-SOL side of the pool (the token being bought/sold).

is_buy.description: `true` if SOL → token; `false` if token → SOL.

token_amount.description: Token base units; divide by 10**`quote_decimals` for human-readable.

fee_bps.description: Effective LP fee, in basis points.

creator_fee.description: Creator-set fee paid, in lamports (pump.fun + meteora-dbc).

price_per_token.description: Realized fill price (SOL per 1 token, decimals-adjusted).

price_impact_bps.description: Difference between fill price and pre-swap mid, in basis points.

virtual_sol_reserves.description: Virtual SOL reserves (pump.fun bonding curve). 0 on non-pump pools.

real_sol_reserves.description: Actual reserves on the pool after the swap.

transfer_fee_in.description: Token-2022 transfer-fee paid on the input side (lamports/base units).

SwapsPage.description: Paged list of swaps, shared by `/swaps`, `/swaps/range`, `/swaps/whale`.

total.description: Count of swaps in this page.

has_more.description: True if more rows match — increment `offset` to page.

GlobalStats.description: Lifetime totals across the entire index (not a 24h window).

TraderStats.description: Lifetime stats for a wallet across all pools and DEXs.

total_sol_volume.description: Cumulative SOL volume routed by this wallet.

unique_tokens.description: Number of distinct token mints this wallet has traded.

TokenStats.description: Lifetime stats for a token mint across every indexed pool.

TopMover.description: One pool in a top-movers response. The `gainers` and `losers` arrays each contain these.

mint.description: Token mint (non-SOL side of the pool).

volume_sol.description: SOL volume over `timeframe`. **Lamports** despite the name — divide by 1e9.

TrendingResult.description: One token in a /trending response. Volume and liquidity are in USD via the Pyth SOL/USD oracle.

change_pct.description: Price change percent over the trending `window`. `null` when the window contains only one swap or the start price was zero.

trending_score.description: Composite score (only present when `include_score=true`). Higher = more momentum.

formula_breakdown.description: Per-component contribution to `trending_score`. Only present when `include_score=true`. All components sum to `trending_score`.

vol_acceleration.description: `ln(vol_now / vol_prior) * 4`, clamped to `[-8, 12]`. Brand-new mints with no prior-window activity get a fixed `+4` boost so first-window tokens can surface. This is the dominant momentum term.

buyer_acceleration.description: `((buyers_now - buyers_prior) / max(buyers_prior, 1)) * 2`, clamped to `[-2, 6]`. Percent-change in unique buyers vs the prior window.

dev_sell_penalty.description: Negative. `-min(dev_sell_sol * 0.5, 20)` — capped subtraction per SOL sold by the token's creator.

holder_growth.description: Reserved for future use. Always `null` today.

top10_penalty.description: Reserved for future use. Always `null` today.

change_pct_by_window.description: Populated only when `extra_windows=...` was set on the request. Keys are the window strings you asked for (e.g. `"5m"`, `"24h"`); values are the price change percent for that window, or `null` if no price reference at window start.

PoolEvent.description: Liquidity-pool event other than a swap — LP deposit, LP withdraw, token burn, or a server-classified rug. The real-time push of these same events is the `pool_event` frame on the [`/ws/swaps`](/streaming/websocket) WebSocket: a large `lp_withdraw` is the live LP-removal signal, and `rug_detected` is its classified version (`RUGGED` / `LIQUIDITY_DRAINING`).

lp_token_amount.description: DAMM v2 saturates this to u64::MAX — prefer `base_amount` + `quote_amount` for math.

drained_pct.description: Fraction of pool liquidity removed by this event, 0..1. Constant-product pools use the LP-supply ratio; Meteora CL uses the SOL-reserve ratio. `0.0` when not computable.

classification.description: Set only on `rug_detected` events: `RUGGED` when >95% of liquidity was removed, `LIQUIDITY_DRAINING` when 50–95%. Empty otherwise.

pool_base_reserve_after.description: Post-event pool reserve, base side (atomic). `0` when N/A.

pool_quote_reserve_after.description: Post-event pool reserve, quote side (lamports for SOL-quoted pools). `0` when N/A.

lp_supply_after.description: Post-event total LP supply. Constant-product only — `0` for Meteora.

TokenAudit.description:
On-chain authority and LP-burn checks for a token, served by a dedicated service (CT106) at the **root** path `/tokens/{mint}/audit` (not under `/api/v1`).

A valid base58 mint that has not been indexed yet still returns **HTTP 200** with `indexed: false` and every flag/value `null` (the unindexed sentinel) — it does not 404. A malformed (non-base58) mint returns **HTTP 400**.

indexed.description: `false` when the mint is valid but not yet indexed — in that case `mint_revoked`, `freeze_revoked`, `lp_burnt`, and `computed_at` are all `null` and `top10_pct.status` is `no_holder_data`. `true` once audit data has been computed.

lp_burnt.description: True if the dominant LP token is burnt/locked. `null` when not indexed/computed. This is the boolean roll-up; see `lock` for the structured signal.

lock.description: Structured LP-lock signal. `null` when there is no structured signal yet (same meaning as a `null` `lp_burnt`). When present it distinguishes *how* the LP is secured and how confident we are.

secured_pct.description: Fraction of LP secured, `0..1`. Precise when measured by the polling detector; `null` when only the real-time firehose event path observed the lock (a binary on-chain burn/lock event — exact share not measured). Never fabricated.

mechanism.description: `burned` = LP sent to the incinerator (irreversible). `locked` = LP/position held by a locker program (Streamflow / Jupiter Lock / Raydium Burn&Earn / UNCX). `partial` / `unsecured` are graded readings from the detector. Bonding-curve venues (PumpFun, Meteora DBC) have no fungible LP to burn, so `lock` stays `null` there.

verified.description: `true` for direct on-chain observations (firehose LP burn/lock events; Raydium AMM/CPMM verified layouts). `false` for asserted layouts (Meteora DAMM v2, Raydium CLMM position scans).

computed_at.description: Unix epoch seconds. `null` when not indexed/computed.

TokenMetadata.description: Token metadata as indexed from on-chain + the Metaplex URI. Audit flags are inlined.

image_url_cached.description: Dexploit-hosted mirror; safe to embed without CORS pain.

supply_atomic.description: Base-unit supply; divide by 10**decimals for human-readable.

TokenIntelEntry.description: One entry in the `snipers`, `insiders`, `top_70`, or `dev` arrays.

TokenIntel.description:
Aggregated intelligence for one token mint: actor aggregates, holders, price/volume trend, dev concentration, and an embedded Risk Score summary. Cached server-side ~60s.

Full per-actor lists live at dedicated endpoints (e.g. `/tokens/{mint}/snipers`); the `actors` object here carries counts + concentration percentages with `fetch_url` deep-links.

holders_count.description: Distinct wallets currently holding non-zero balance.

creator_held_pct.description: Percent of supply still held by the creator wallet (0–100).

bonding_pct.description: Pump.fun bonding-curve progress (0–100); null for graduated/non-pump tokens.

dev.description: Creator wallet, source provenance, and recent launches. `null` when the creator is unknown (Phase 0 strict precedence — never falls back to largest holder).

change_pct.description: Price change percent across canonical windows.

dev_held_lp_pct.description: Percent of LP tokens still held by the creator (when LP is not burnt).

Top10Pct.description: Top 10 holders' share of supply, with explicit status. Always present (even when the value isn't computable yet).

value.description: Top-10 (pool-excluded) sum divided by total supply. `null` when `status` is not `ready`.

status.description: Snake-case status of the top-10 computation. `value`/`computed_at` are non-null only when `ready`.

ActorAggregates.description: Per-tag count + concentration of wallets currently holding the mint. Keys are tag names (`sniper`, `insider`, `smart_money`, `whale`, `kol`, `fresh`, `dev`, `bot`) — only tags with at least one matching holder appear. Use `fetch_url` to deep-link to the full list endpoint.

pct_of_circulating.description: Sum of these wallets' balances divided by total supply (0.0–1.0).

fetch_url.description: Path to the full list endpoint. Present only for actor types that have a dedicated endpoint (sniper, insider, smart_money, whale).

RiskSummary.description: Slim Risk Score summary embedded in `/intel`. Full per-signal breakdown at `/tokens/{mint}/risk`.

score.description: 0.0–10.0; null when no input data is available yet.

signals_summary.description: Up to 5 codes of the highest-weighted signals that fired.

next_cursor.description: Opaque base64 cursor. Pass back as `?cursor=...`; `null` means end-of-results.

balance_atomic.description: u64 as decimal string for JSON safety.

rank.description: 1-based position in this page (resumed across pages by cursor).

RiskResponse.description: Glass-box token risk: a 0–10 score plus the per-signal breakdown that produced it. See [Risk Score algorithm](/concepts/risk-score) for the full signal taxonomy.

signals.description: Only signals that triggered are included. To re-compute the score: `min(10, sum(signal.contribution) * 10 / 5000)`.

max_raw.description: 53500 — the sum of every signal's weight.

missing_signals.description: Codes of signals whose input data wasn't available yet (mint not fully onboarded).

value.description: Concrete measurement; shape varies per signal.

contribution.description: weight × graded_strength; equals `weight` for boolean signals, scaled for graded ones.

label.description: Human-readable description of what this signal observed.

computed_at.description: Unix epoch seconds; 0 if never computed.

supply_atomic.description: u64 as decimal string for JSON safety.

creator.description: Phase 0 strict precedence — null when unknown.

graduated_at.description: Unix epoch seconds of the graduated lifecycle event.

results.description: Keyed by mint. Value is null when the mint is not in the tokens table.

ts_ms.description: Unix epoch ms of the source candle.

results.description: Keyed by mint. Null when the mint has no recent (last-hour) candle.

CreditsResponse.description: RPS-tier usage snapshot for the authenticated API key.

monthly_used.description: Cumulative request count this calendar month. Informational only — no cap.

monthly_used_note.example: informational; no monthly cap on Dexploit

WalletTagCounts.description: Per-tag count of wallets currently holding a given token. Useful for surfacing 'sniper-heavy' or 'whale-heavy' tokens.

PumpLifecycleEvent.description: A point in a pump.fun token's lifecycle: mint, bonding-curve progress, or migration to pumpswap.

event_type.description: `graduated` fires once the bonding curve completes and the token migrates to a tradeable AMM pool.

AthResponse.description: Per-mint all-time high. All ATH fields are nullable — they're null when the mint has no daily candles in `ohlcv_1d` yet.

ath_price_usd.description: `ath_price_sol × current Pyth SOL/USD`. Today-equivalent USD value, not the USD price at the moment of the ATH.

ath_ts_ms.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when the ATH occurred.

ath_pair_address.description: Pair where the ATH candle was observed.

DeployerResponse.description: Tokens deployed by a wallet. `coverage_note` is only present when `results` is empty.

results.description: Newest mint first. Same shape as `/tokens/multi`.

coverage_note.description: Set when results is empty. Explains that creator capture began 2026-05-26 (Phase 0).

PriceHistoryPoint.description: Single OHLCV row. `volume_sol` is **lamports** (divide by 1e9 for SOL).

volume_sol.description: **Lamports**, despite the name. Divide by 1e9 for SOL.

PriceHistorySeries.description: Candle series for a token's top-volume pair.

pair_address.description: The pair the series was sourced from.

PriceHistoryRange.description: Single aggregated OHLCV row over `[from_ts, to_ts]`.

volume_sol.description: Sum of candle `volume_sol` (lamports). Divide by 1e9 for SOL.

ChartResponse.description: Chart-ready candle series with optional per-row market cap.

BundlersStubResponse.description: Phase 2 stub. Real bundler detection ships in Phase 5; this endpoint currently always returns `count: 0` and `status: "tagger_pending"`.

ActorChartSinglePoint.description: One point in a single-series actor chart. `value` is an integer count for holders/snipers/insiders/bundlers.

value.description: Point-in-time count for the requested series.

ActorChartSingleResponse.description: Response for a single-series actor chart endpoint (`/holders/chart/{token}`, `/snipers/chart/{token}`, `/insiders/chart/{token}`, `/bundlers/chart/{token}`). Rows exist only for hours the mint traded — gaps are expected, not bugs. Forward-fill the last value for quiet hours. An empty `results` array carries a `coverage_note` explaining the no-backfill policy.

computed_at.description: Unix ms when this response was assembled.

coverage_note.description: Present on empty results (no-backfill policy) or for the bundlers endpoint (Phase 5 placeholder).

ActorChartCombinedPoint.description: One wide row from the combined actor-chart endpoint. Keys present depend on the `?series=` subset requested. `dev_held_pct` is a 0.0–1.0 fraction.

holders.description: Distinct holder count at this timestamp. Present when `holders` is in the requested series.

snipers.description: Sniper wallet count. Present when `snipers` is in the requested series.

insiders.description: Insider wallet count. Present when `insiders` is in the requested series.

dev_held_pct.description: Developer's share of supply (0.0–1.0 fraction). Present when `dev` is in the requested series.

ActorChartCombinedResponse.description: Response for the combined actor-chart endpoint (`/tokens/{mint}/actor-chart`). Wide rows — one object per timestamp with all requested series as keys. One call replaces up to four single-series requests.

computed_at.description: Unix ms when this response was assembled.

coverage_note.description: Present on empty results — no pre-launch backfill; charts grow forward from Phase 3 launch (2026-05-28).

Holding.description: One token position in a wallet holdings response. `cost_basis_sol`, `unrealized_pnl_sol`, and `current_value_usd` are `null` when not computable (see `no_cost_basis` and `price_stale`).

qty.description: Current quantity in token base units.

cost_basis_sol.description: FIFO average cost in SOL, scaled to the current on-chain qty. `null` when no swap history is available for this mint.

no_cost_basis.description: `true` when the mint has no swap history in Dexploit's index (airdrop, off-platform transfer, etc.).

current_value_sol.description: Current market value in SOL (latest candle price × qty). `null` when price is unavailable.

current_value_usd.description: Current market value in USD via the Pyth SOL/USD oracle. `null` when price or oracle is unavailable.

unrealized_pnl_sol.description: `current_value_sol − cost_basis_sol`. `null` when either value is unavailable.

price_stale.description: `true` when no recent candle price was found for this mint (no trade in the last hour).

HoldingsResponse.description: Response from `/wallets/{w}/holdings`. `holdings_source` indicates whether the token list came from Shyft (authoritative) or fell back to FIFO-derived data.

holdings_source.description: `shyft` = authoritative on-chain balances. `fifo_fallback` = Shyft was temporarily unavailable; list is derived from Dexploit's swap index only.

computed_at.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when this response was assembled.

HoldingsBasicResponse.description: Response from `/wallets/{w}/holdings/basic`. FIFO-derived holdings from Dexploit's own swap index — no Shyft call, no USD values. Fast (~30 ms). Only covers tokens traded through indexed DEXes.

Trade.description: One trade in a wallet's history. `pnl_delta_sol` is the FIFO realized gain/loss on sells; `null` on buys.

is_buy.description: `true` if SOL → token; `false` if token → SOL.

sol_amount.description: SOL value of the swap (already divided by 1e9 from lamports).

pnl_delta_sol.description: FIFO realized PnL in SOL on this sell (`proceeds − matched_cost`). Always `null` on buys.

TradesResponse.description: Cursor-paginated trade history for a wallet, newest first.

next_before.description: Pass as `?before=` on the next request to get the next page. `null` when there are no more trades.

coverage_note.description: Always present in Phase 4. Explains that `hide_arb` and `parse_jupiter` are no-ops until Phase 5.

PortfolioChartPoint.description: One day in a wallet portfolio chart series.

value_sol.description: Portfolio value in SOL on this day, computed from historical `ohlcv_1d` closes.

value_usd.description: Portfolio value in USD. Uses the **current** SOL/USD rate for all historical points — Dexploit does not store a daily SOL/USD history. Apply your own historical SOL/USD to `value_sol` for accurate historical USD.

provisional.description: Present and `true` only on `today` — the current day is computed live and has not been finalized.

PortfolioChartResponse.description: Daily portfolio value series for a wallet. Closed days are immutable and served from the store; today is always computed live.

series.description: Closed days only. Each point is immutable once written.

today.description: Today's portfolio value, always computed live. `provisional: true` is always set.

computed_at.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when this response was assembled.

PnlBasicResponse.description: FIFO realized + unrealized PnL for a wallet. Includes a `warning` pointing to Phase 8 for full multi-lot PnL.

realized_sol.description: Total FIFO realized PnL in SOL across all closed positions.

unrealized_sol.description: Unrealized PnL in SOL on remaining open lots.

win_rate.description: Fraction of sells that were profitable (0.0–1.0).

realized_usd.description: Realized PnL in USD via the Pyth oracle. `null` when oracle unavailable.

unrealized_usd.description: Unrealized PnL in USD via the Pyth oracle. `null` when oracle unavailable.

oracle.description: Pyth SOL/USD oracle snapshot used for USD conversion.

warning.description: Always present. Explains that this is basic FIFO PnL and that full cost-basis ships in Phase 8.

SolBalanceResponse.description: Native SOL balance for a wallet. `sol_balance` is `null` when Shyft's `getBalance` call fails; the response is still 200.

sol_balance.description: Native SOL balance (not lamports). `null` on Shyft error.

sol_balance_usd.description: SOL balance converted to USD via the Pyth oracle. `null` when balance or oracle is unavailable.

computed_at.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when this response was assembled.

WalletStatsResponse.description: Aggregate trading statistics for a wallet over a given window.

win_count.description: Number of sells that were profitable.

win_rate.description: Fraction of sells that were profitable (0.0–1.0).

best_trade_pnl_sol.description: Realized PnL of the single most profitable sell, in SOL.

worst_trade_pnl_sol.description: Realized PnL of the single worst sell, in SOL.

OracleSnapshot.description: Pyth SOL/USD oracle snapshot used for USD conversion. `null` when the oracle is unavailable.

RealizedBreakdown.description: Realized PnL (SOL) under each accounting mode. Present on glass-box responses or when `breakdown=1`.

PnlPosition.description: A single (wallet, mint) cost-basis position. All `*_sol` fields are already SOL (lamports/1e9). `avg_cost_per_token` and any spot price are lamports per **atomic** token unit. `first_trade_ts`/`last_trade_ts` are epoch **milliseconds**.

mint.description: Token mint (omitted on token-keyed variants where `wallet` is present instead).

balance_atomic.description: Current open balance in atomic token units (int64).

avg_cost_per_token.description: Average cost in lamports per atomic token unit.

realized_sol.description: Realized PnL in SOL for the selected `pnl_mode`.

unrealized_sol.description: Read-time spot unrealized PnL in SOL. `null` for null-priced mints.

current_value_sol.description: Read-time spot value of the open balance, in SOL. `null` when unpriced.

fastest_flip_seconds.description: Shortest buy→sell round-trip in seconds (sentinel 0 = no flip).

first_trade_ts.description: Epoch milliseconds of the first trade in this position.

last_trade_ts.description: Epoch milliseconds of the most recent trade.

is_arbitrageur.description: Whether this (wallet, mint) was tagged as habitual cross-venue arbitrage.

realized_usd.description: Realized PnL in USD via the Pyth oracle.

realized_breakdown.description: Only present on glass-box endpoints (single-token, batch, traders) or when `breakdown=1`.

PnlPositionByWallet.description: Same shape as `PnlPosition` but keyed on `wallet` (the `mint` key is removed and `wallet` inserted). Returned by token-scoped endpoints.

PnlV2Summary.description: Cost-basis PnL summary for a wallet (Phase 8 engine). All `*_sol` fields are SOL. `unrealized_sol` is read-time spot Σ(price−avg_cost)*balance; null-priced mints contribute 0. Wallets with no positions return zeros and an empty `positions` array.

unrealized_sol.description: Read-time spot unrealized PnL (null-priced mints contribute 0).

open_positions.description: Count of positions with balance > 0 and not a quote mint.

LeaderboardRow.description: One ranked wallet on a realized-PnL leaderboard.

KolLeaderboardRow.description: One ranked KOL wallet (adds Twitter/display metadata).

KolCalendarRow.description: One KOL row in the realized-only heatmap. `cells` is sparse — one cell per active day, carrying that day's realized DELTA.

TokenTraderRow.description: One trader of a token, with mode-selected realized PnL plus the full breakdown.

FirstBuyerRow.description: An early buyer of a token, ordered by (first_buy_ts, first_slot, first_sig).

pnl.description: Present only when `enrich=1`. Zeroed (`{realized_sol:0, balance_atomic:0, unrealized_sol:null}`) for buyers with no tracked position.

PnlHighlight.description: A standout position. `null` when no qualifying row matched.

ScreenerResultV2.description: Full Phase-7 V2 screener result row (`compact=false`).

price_per_token.description: Price in **lamports per atomic token unit** (1 SOL = 1e9 lamports).

last_ts.description: Epoch ms of most recent swap considered.

first_ts.description: Epoch ms of first swap in the window.

liquidity_sol.description: Pool/curve liquidity in SOL. A **number** when `liquidity_status` is `IN_CURVE`; **0** when `DRAINED` (curve reserves collapsed — LP-drain/rug); **null** for `POOL_DEPTH` (graduated/AMM pools, where curve liquidity is not the relevant measure).

liquidity_status.description: Source/meaning of `liquidity_sol`. `IN_CURVE` = pre-graduation bonding-curve liquidity (numeric `liquidity_sol`); `POOL_DEPTH` = post-graduation AMM pool depth (`liquidity_sol` is null); `DRAINED` = curve reserves collapsed / rugged (`liquidity_sol` is `0`).

market_cap.description: Market cap in SOL. **null** (not 0) when not yet computable.

holder_count.description: uint64. **null** (not 0) when holder data has not been computed for this mint yet.

top10_pct.description: Top-10 (pool-excluded) share of supply. **null** (not 0) when holder data is unavailable.

ScreenerResultV2Compact.description: Compact Phase-7 V2 screener result row (`compact=true`).

price_per_token.description: Price in lamports per atomic token unit (1 SOL = 1e9 lamports).

ScreenerFiltersV2.description: Phase-7 V2 screener filters (~40 fields, all optional, combined as AND). Replaces the legacy 6-field `ScreenerFilters`. Range filters take `{min, max}`. Volume/change windows default to `1h`.

mint.description: Single mint address. Must be a valid base58 mint; a malformed value returns `400 invalid parameter: invalid_filter:mint` (see endpoint responses).

mints.description: Batch filter — restrict results to this set of mints (cap 200). Use this to fetch screener rows for a known list of tokens in one call. Any malformed entry returns `400 invalid parameter: invalid_filter:mints`.

creator.description: Creator/deployer wallet. A malformed (non-base58) value returns `400 invalid parameter: invalid_filter:creator`.

risk_score.description: Deferred — currently produces a warning rather than filtering.

get.description:
List on-chain pools indexed by Dexploit. **Response shape varies with input:**

- **No `token_address`** (or only `protocol`): `data` is a flat array of `pair_address` strings — the cheapest way to enumerate pools.
- **With `token_address`** (or `token_addresses`): `data` is an array of `Pair` objects with `protocol` and `recent_volume_sol`, sorted by recent SOL volume — the discovery path for the `pair_address` you'll pass to candle and swap endpoints.

parameters.name: token_address
parameters.description: Token mint to find pools for. When set, the response is sorted by recent SOL volume.

parameters.name: token_addresses
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of token mints (max 200). Same enriched response as `token_address`.

200.description: List of pools. Shape depends on whether `token_address`/`token_addresses` was supplied (see endpoint description).

get.description:
Returns OHLCV bars for `pair_address` between `start` and `end`. Bars are built from real swap events — see [Swap-event OHLCV](/concepts/swap-event-ohlcv).

**Requires `pair_address`.** Sending `mint`, `pair`, or `protocol` returns 400 with a pointer to /api/v1/pairs.

400.description: Missing pair_address or sent legacy mint/pair/protocol

error.message: use pair_address; see /api/v1/pairs?token_address=X to discover pools

get.summary: Most recent candles for a pool
get.description:
Returns the most-recent N candles for `pair_address`.

**Requires `pair_address`.** The legacy `protocol=&pair=` form is no longer accepted — sending `protocol`/`pair` (or `mint`) returns **HTTP 400** `invalid parameter: use pair_address` (discover the pool via [`/api/v1/pairs?token_address=`](#tag/pairs/get/api/v1/pairs)).

400.description: Missing `pair_address`, or sent the legacy `protocol`/`pair` form (→ `invalid parameter: use pair_address`).

get.description: Returns recent swaps with optional filters. Use this for a real-time-ish polling view; for true real-time, use [WebSocket](/streaming/websocket) or [gRPC](/streaming/grpc).

parameters.name: min_sol
parameters.description: Minimum SOL amount (decimal SOL, not lamports).

parameters.name: signature
parameters.description: Restrict to one Solana tx signature.

get.summary: Swaps in a time or slot range
get.description: Page through historical swaps within a `[start_time, end_time]` (Unix epoch **seconds**) or `[start_slot, end_slot]` range. Combine with the same filters as [/swaps](#tag/swaps/get/swaps).
Returns recent swaps with `sol_amount` above a threshold (default 10 SOL).
Look up the swap event emitted by a specific Solana transaction. Returns the swap object directly (no envelope).

get.summary: Swap history for a trader (with lifetime stats)
get.description: All swaps a wallet has made, across pools and DEXs, plus the same `TraderStats` block returned by [/stats/trader/{wallet}](#tag/stats-trends/get/stats/trader/{wallet}) — saves a second round-trip when rendering a wallet page.

get.summary: Swap history for a token (with lifetime stats)
get.description: Returns swaps for a token across **every** pool we index, plus a `TokenStats` block. This is one of the few endpoints where token mint (not `pair_address`) is the right input — it intentionally aggregates. See [Pairs vs tokens](/concepts/pairs-vs-tokens).
Aggregate lifetime stats across the entire index. (Not a 24h window — these are cumulative since `data_inception` reported by [/api/v1/stats](#tag/stats-trends/get/api/v1/stats).)
Lifetime stats for a single wallet. Returns the `TraderStats` block directly.

Aggregate lifetime stats for a token mint across every indexed pool. Returns the `TokenStats` block directly. One of the few endpoints where token mint is correct (see [Pairs vs tokens](/concepts/pairs-vs-tokens)).

Tokens with the highest momentum over the requested window. The score compares the current window to the prior equal-length window, so a token that suddenly accelerates ranks above a steady high-volume token. Brand-new mints with no prior-window activity get a fast-riser boost. Pass `include_score=true` to expose the per-component `formula_breakdown` so you can re-sort client-side.

**Rug filter:** tokens whose price collapsed by 90% or more inside the window are excluded by default — their activity is rug-watchers piling in, not organic demand. Set `include_rugged=true` to opt in.

**Pagination:** use `offset` + `limit` to page through the scored list. The response carries `total` (how many candidates were scored before slicing), `offset`, and `limit` so you can detect end-of-list. Pricing/liquidity are USD via the Pyth SOL/USD oracle.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size — number of results to return.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Page offset — skip the first N scored results. Combined with `limit` for stable-cache-window pagination.

parameters.name: include_rugged
parameters.description: Include tokens whose price dropped by 90% or more inside the window. Default `false`.

parameters.name: extra_windows
parameters.description: Comma-separated list (max 3) of additional windows to populate `change_pct_by_window` on each result (e.g. `5m,24h`). Each extra window is one parallel ClickHouse query, so don't ask for more than you'll use.

total.description: Scored candidates before pagination slicing. When `offset + limit >= total`, you've reached the end.

offset.description: Echo of the effective `offset` (0 if unset).

get.description: Pools with the largest absolute price change over the requested timeframe. Returns *both* sides: `gainers` (sorted by largest positive change) and `losers` (sorted by largest negative change).

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: Window over which to compute price change.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Per side — you get up to `limit` gainers AND up to `limit` losers.

error.message: Failed to deserialize query string: missing field `timeframe`

get.description: Returns the lowercase snake_case names of every DEX Dexploit currently indexes. These are the values you'll pass as `protocol=` to [/api/v1/pairs](#tag/pairs/get/api/v1/pairs) and [/api/v1/top-movers](#tag/stats-trends/get/api/v1/top-movers), or as `dex=` to [/swaps](#tag/swaps/get/swaps).
Roll-up of the OHLCV index — how many candles, pools, mints, and supported timeframes; plus when the index started.

data_inception.description: When Dexploit started ingesting Solana swaps.

get.description: Fetch `/stats/token/{mint}` for up to 200 mints in one round-trip. Returned object is keyed by mint.

get.summary: LP and burn events for a token
get.description:
Non-swap pool activity for a token: LP deposits, LP withdrawals, token burns, and server-classified `rug_detected` events across every indexed pool.

Useful for detecting rug-pulls (large `lp_withdraw` events, or a `rug_detected` with `classification` `RUGGED`/`LIQUIDITY_DRAINING`), tracking dev positions, or surfacing freshly-locked liquidity.

For the real-time push of these same events, subscribe to the `pool_event` frame on the [`/ws/swaps`](/streaming/websocket) WebSocket.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Cursor — epoch ms; returns events strictly older than this.

post.description:
Server-side filtered + sorted token search. Filters combine as AND. Volume/change windows default to `1h`.

The NL-style query bar and chip bar on the Dexploit terminal screener serialize into this same JSON body. The flat `GET /search` alias maps query-string params onto the same filters.

Response is **top-level** — `{ results, total_matches, limit, offset, warnings }` with **no** `{success, data, error}` wrapper. (Errors are still enveloped — see below.)

Response headers: `x-cache` (`HIT`|`MISS`) and `x-total-matches-exact` (`true`|`false` — when `false`, `total_matches` is a lower bound). `warnings` carries deferred-filter and cold-cache notes (e.g. `sectors_filter_skipped_cold_cache`, `total_matches_is_lower_bound`, and risk_score being deferred).

**Two failure modes:**
- **Malformed address filter** (`mint`, `mints[]`, or `creator` not valid base58) → **HTTP 400** with the enveloped body `{"success":false,"data":null,"error":"invalid parameter: invalid_filter:mint|creator|mints"}`.
- **Unsupported filter** (e.g. `risk_score`) → stays **HTTP 200**; the filter is ignored and a note is appended to the `warnings[]` array rather than failing the request.

The `GET /search` alias inherits both behaviors.

compact.description: When `true`, rows use the compact variant.

total_matches.description: Total matching tokens before paging. See `x-total-matches-exact`.

400.description: Malformed address filter (`mint`, `mints[]`, or `creator` not valid base58). Unsupported filters like `risk_score` do **not** 400 — they stay 200 with a `warnings[]` note.

error.description: Always of the form `invalid parameter: invalid_filter:<field>` where `<field>` is `mint`, `creator`, or `mints`.

get.description: Resolve metadata + audit flags for up to 200 mints in one call. This is the right endpoint for hydrating a list view — don't loop over `/tokens/{mint}`.
Metadata + audit for one mint. Includes the Dexploit-hosted `image_url_cached` mirror.

On-chain authority + LP-burn rug-check signals for a token. Cheaper than `/tokens/{mint}` when you only need audit flags.

**Served by a separate service (CT106) at the root path** — `https://api.dexploit.dev/tokens/{mint}/audit`, *not* under `/api/v1`.

**Response semantics:**
- Valid base58 mint, **indexed** → `200` with `indexed: true` and populated flags.
- Valid base58 mint, **not yet indexed** → `200` with the unindexed sentinel: `indexed: false`, all flags `null`, and `top10_pct: {value: null, status: "no_holder_data", computed_at: null}`. It does **not** 404.
- **Malformed** (non-base58) mint → `400`.

200.description: Audit flags. `indexed: false` (with all-null flags) is the sentinel for a valid-but-unindexed mint — not an error.

get.description:
Per-mint dashboard payload: actor aggregates, top-10 holder concentration, dev info, price/liquidity context, and an embedded Risk Score summary. Server-side cached ~60s.

For the full holder list use [`/tokens/{mint}/holders`](#tag/tokens/get/tokens/{mint}/holders); for actor lists deep-link via the `fetch_url` in each `actors[*]` entry; for the full per-signal Risk breakdown call [`/tokens/{mint}/risk`](#tag/tokens/get/tokens/{mint}/risk).

Wallets currently holding non-zero balance of `{mint}`, sorted by balance descending. Cursor-paginated (default 100, max 200).

Pass `?enrich=identity` to populate the `tags[]` field on each row from `wallet_tags` (e.g. `sniper`, `insider`, `smart_money`, `whale`). Enrichment adds a JOIN; expect ~30% higher cold-path latency.

parameters.name: enrich
parameters.description: Comma-separated enrichment tokens. Currently only `identity` is recognized.

get.description: Same shape as `/tokens/{mint}/holders` but fixed at 20 rows and no cursor — ideal for a 'top holders' panel.

Wallets tagged `sniper` for this specific mint who currently hold non-zero balance. A sniper is a wallet that bought within the first 30 slots of the mint's first swap.

Cursor-paginated (default 50, max 200).

Wallets tagged `insider` for this specific mint who currently hold non-zero balance. An insider is a wallet that received tokens but has never appeared as a swapper on-DEX (e.g. via airdrop or bonding-curve purchase outside of `pump_swaps`).

Cursor-paginated (default 50, max 200).

Wallets globally tagged `smart_money` (high historical PnL across many mints) who currently hold non-zero balance of `{mint}`.

**Tier-gated**: Free tier is capped at 100 results per UTC day per endpoint. Paid tiers bypass the cap.

Cursor-paginated (default 50, max 200).

Wallets globally tagged `whale` who currently hold non-zero balance of `{mint}`.

**Tier-gated**: Free tier is capped at 100 results per UTC day per endpoint. Paid tiers bypass the cap.

Cursor-paginated (default 50, max 200).

0.0–10.0 rug-pull risk score with the full per-signal breakdown that produced it. Glass-box — every signal that fired is in `signals[]` with its `code`, `weight`, `value`, and `contribution`. You can re-compute the score yourself: `min(10, sum(s.contribution for s in signals) * 10 / 5000)`.

See [Risk Score algorithm](/concepts/risk-score) for the full signal taxonomy.

Per-mint all-time high across every pair the mint has traded in. Computed continuously via a `maxState(close) + argMaxState(timestamp, close) + argMaxState(pair_address, close)` Aggregating MV over `ohlcv_1d`. `ath_price_usd` is the SOL ATH multiplied by the current Pyth SOL/USD price — it's a today-equivalent USD value, not the USD price at the moment of the ATH.

Returns `ath_price_sol: null` (and the other ATH fields null) when the mint has no daily candles yet.

400.description: Mint is not a valid base58 string

get.description: Phase 2 placeholder. Real bundler detection ships in Phase 5 — once the wallet-tagger pipeline backfills the `bundler` tag, this endpoint will return a count and list of bundler wallets. Today it always returns `count: 0` with `status: "tagger_pending"` and `available_in: "phase_5"`.
Tokens sorted by `tokens.created_at DESC`. Use this to surface fresh mints across all supported DEXes. For pump.fun-specific filters see `/tokens/multi/graduating` and `/tokens/multi/graduated`.
Identical to `/tokens/latest` — alias for API compatibility with Solana Tracker.
Active pump.fun bonding curves with `bonding_pct >= 80` that have NOT yet emitted a `graduated` lifecycle event. Sorted by `bonding_pct DESC`. Useful for sniping the migration window.
Pump.fun mints with a `graduated` event in `pump_lifecycle_events`, sorted by graduation timestamp DESC.

post.description: Look up metadata for up to 20 mints in a single call. Response is keyed by mint; unknown mints map to `null` so the client can tell missing-from-DB from missing-in-request.

get.description: Latest ohlcv_1m close × Pyth SOL/USD per mint. Up to 100 mints. Mints with no recent (last-hour) candle return as `null` in the results map.

get.summary: Historical price series for a token
get.description:
OHLCV candles for the token's top-volume pair. The pair is resolved automatically — pass `?token={mint}` and the handler picks the busiest pool. For explicit pair-level access use [GET /api/v1/candles](/api-reference/openapi/get/api/v1/candles).

Default resolution is `1m`. See [Timeframes & resolution](/concepts/timeframes-resolution) for the resolution → MV mapping.

200.description: Candle series for the token's top pair

get.description:
Returns the single most-recent 1m candle at or before `timestamp` (Unix ms) for the token's top-volume pair. Lookback window is 60 seconds — older queries return 404.

Resolution is fixed at `1m` (the highest-precision MV).

404.description: No pair found for token, or no candle within the 60s lookback window

get.summary: Aggregated OHLCV over an arbitrary range
get.description:
Single aggregated OHLCV row spanning `[from, to]` (Unix ms) for the token's top pair. Internally reads candles at the requested `resolution` (default `1m`) and rolls them up: `open` = first candle open, `close` = last candle close, `high`/`low` = max/min across the range, `volume_sol` = sum, `sample_count` = number of candles aggregated.

Returns 400 if the window expands to more than 10,000 candles at the chosen resolution — bump to a coarser resolution or shrink the window.

400.description: Invalid token, `from > to`, unsupported resolution, or range exceeds 10k candles

404.description: No pair found for token, or no candles in range

get.summary: Chart-ready candle series with market cap
get.description:
Candle series for the token's top-volume pair, optionally enriched with per-row `market_cap_sol` (= `close × current_supply_atomic / 10^decimals`). Use this when rendering a market-cap-aware chart without round-tripping a supply lookup yourself.

`historicalSupply=true` is accepted but currently ignored — historical supply tracking from `tokens_supply_history` ships in Phase 5. When `marketCap=false` (the default), `market_cap_sol` is `null` on every row.

parameters.name: marketCap
parameters.description: When true, populate `market_cap_sol` on every row.

parameters.name: historicalSupply
parameters.description: Reserved for Phase 5. Accepted but ignored today.

get.summary: Holder-count time series for a token
get.description:
Returns the distinct holder count for a token sampled once per hour (or per 6h/1d bucket). Data is event-driven — a row exists only for hours the mint traded. Quiet hours produce gaps, not zeros; frontends should forward-fill the last value.

No pre-launch backfill: history starts at Phase 3 launch (2026-05-28) and grows forward. Empty mints return `results: []` with a `coverage_note`.

Downsampling at `6h`/`1d` returns the **last value in the bucket** (point-in-time count, not a flow).

Data freshness is ~3–5 seconds after a trade.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of range (Unix ms, inclusive). Default: 24h before `to`.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of range (Unix ms, inclusive). Default: now.

400.description: Invalid token mint, unsupported resolution, or `from > to`

get.summary: Sniper-count time series for a token
get.description:
Returns the sniper wallet count for a token over time. Snipers are wallets that bought within the first few blocks of a mint's launch, as tagged by Dexploit's wallet-intel pipeline.

Same sparsity and freshness semantics as [GET /holders/chart/{token}](/api-reference/openapi/get/holders/chart/{token}): rows exist only for hours the mint traded; gaps are expected; forward-fill for display. No pre-launch backfill.

Downsampling at `6h`/`1d` returns the **last value in the bucket**.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of range (Unix ms, inclusive). Default: 24h before `to`.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of range (Unix ms, inclusive). Default: now.

400.description: Invalid token mint, unsupported resolution, or `from > to`

get.summary: Insider-count time series for a token
get.description:
Returns the insider wallet count for a token over time. Insiders are wallets with an informational edge at launch (pre-launch buys, team/dev-adjacent activity), as tagged by Dexploit's wallet-intel pipeline.

Same sparsity and freshness semantics as [GET /holders/chart/{token}](/api-reference/openapi/get/holders/chart/{token}): rows exist only for hours the mint traded; gaps are expected; forward-fill for display. No pre-launch backfill.

Downsampling at `6h`/`1d` returns the **last value in the bucket**.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of range (Unix ms, inclusive). Default: 24h before `to`.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of range (Unix ms, inclusive). Default: now.

400.description: Invalid token mint, unsupported resolution, or `from > to`

get.summary: Bundler-count time series for a token (stub)
get.description:
Returns the bundler wallet count for a token over time. **All values are 0 until Phase 5** — the bundler tagger has not shipped yet. The response always includes a `coverage_note` explaining this.

The endpoint is available now so you can wire up your UI and swap in real data when Phase 5 ships without a contract change.

Same sparsity semantics as [GET /holders/chart/{token}](/api-reference/openapi/get/holders/chart/{token}). No pre-launch backfill.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of range (Unix ms, inclusive). Default: 24h before `to`.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of range (Unix ms, inclusive). Default: now.

400.description: Invalid token mint, unsupported resolution, or `from > to`

get.summary: Combined actor time series (holders, snipers, insiders, dev)
get.description:
Returns wide rows with up to four actor series in a single call — **one request instead of four**. Each row has `ts_ms` plus whatever series you requested as keys.

Use `?series=holders,snipers,insiders,dev` (comma-separated) to request a subset. Default: all four. The `dev` series returns as `dev_held_pct` (a 0.0–1.0 fraction of supply held by the deployer).

Same sparsity and freshness semantics as the single-series endpoints: rows exist only for hours the mint traded; forward-fill for display. No pre-launch backfill — history starts at Phase 3 launch (2026-05-28).

Downsampling at `6h`/`1d` returns the **last value in the bucket** for each series.

parameters.name: series
parameters.description: Comma-separated subset of series to include. Valid values: `holders`, `snipers`, `insiders`, `dev`. Default: all four.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of range (Unix ms, inclusive). Default: 24h before `to`.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of range (Unix ms, inclusive). Default: now.

400.description: Invalid token mint, unknown series value, unsupported resolution, or `from > to`

get.description: Returns your tier, RPS cap, current 60-second window usage, monthly_used (informational only — no monthly cap on Dexploit), and per-feature gate flags. See [Authentication](/authentication#inspecting-your-usage) for the full payload.
Alias of `/api/v1/billing/subscription`. JWT-authenticated (dashboard session); not callable with an API key. Returns the active Stripe subscription tier + period info.

get.summary: Per-tag count of holders for a token
get.description: How many wallets currently holding this token fall in each tag bucket. Drives the 'sniper count', 'whale count' badges on token cards.

Returns the set of tags Dexploit's wallet-intel pipeline has applied to a wallet (e.g. `sniper`, `insider`, `whale`, `smart_money`). Tags are computed asynchronously — `computed_at: 0` means we haven't profiled this wallet yet.

Lists mints the wallet has deployed, newest first. Reads from the `tokens_by_creator` ReplacingMergeTree (sourced from `tokens.creator`). Results are hydrated into the same `TokenRow` shape as [POST /tokens/multi](/api-reference/openapi/post/tokens/multi).

When there are no results, the response includes `coverage_note` explaining that creator capture began on 2026-05-26 (Phase 0) — older mints may be missing. See [Data coverage](/concepts/coverage).

400.description: Wallet is not a valid base58 string

get.description:
Returns the wallet's current on-chain SPL token holdings sourced from Shyft's `all_tokens` API, with FIFO cost-basis and unrealized PnL overlaid where Dexploit has swap history.

Tokens acquired via airdrop or off-platform transfers will appear with `no_cost_basis: true` and `cost_basis_sol: null`.

When Shyft is temporarily unavailable the endpoint degrades to FIFO-derived holdings and sets `holdings_source: "fifo_fallback"` — it never returns 5xx on a Shyft outage.

Response is cached 30 seconds per wallet. See [Wallet portfolio](/concepts/wallet-portfolio) for a full explanation of the two holdings sources.

parameters.name: dex
parameters.description: Filter FIFO cost-basis computation to a single DEX by integer ID. See `DexId` for the mapping. Has no effect on the Shyft on-chain token list.

400.description: Wallet address is not valid base58

get.description:
Returns holdings derived entirely from Dexploit's own swap index using FIFO lot matching. No external Shyft call — typically ~30 ms.

Only covers tokens the wallet traded through indexed DEXes (pump.fun, pumpswap, Raydium, Meteora, Orca). Tokens acquired via airdrop or off-platform will not appear. No USD values.

Use this endpoint when you need a quick snapshot and completeness is less important than latency. Uncached.

See [Wallet portfolio](/concepts/wallet-portfolio) for the trade-off between this endpoint and `/holdings`.

parameters.name: dex
parameters.description: Filter to a single DEX by integer ID. See `DexId` for the mapping.

400.description: Wallet address is not valid base58

get.summary: Wallet trade history with per-trade PnL
get.description:
Cursor-paginated trade history for a wallet, newest first. Per-trade `pnl_delta_sol` is the FIFO realized gain/loss on sells; `null` on buys.

Filter by `from_token` or `to_token` to scope to a specific mint. For Phase 4 both params filter against the swap's token mint (pump.fun is single-mint-vs-SOL). True two-leg Jupiter routing attribution ships in Phase 5.

`hide_arb` and `parse_jupiter` are accepted but are **no-ops in Phase 4**. The response always includes a `coverage_note` pointing to Phase 5. Wire them into your UI now so they take effect without a contract change.

Response cached 30 seconds.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Cursor — Unix epoch milliseconds. Returns trades strictly older than this timestamp. Use `next_before` from the previous response.

parameters.name: from_token
parameters.description: Filter to trades involving this token mint.

parameters.name: to_token
parameters.description: Filter to trades involving this token mint (alias of `from_token` in Phase 4; full two-leg routing in Phase 5).

parameters.name: dex
parameters.description: Filter to a single DEX by integer ID. See `DexId` for the mapping.

parameters.name: hide_arb
parameters.description: **No-op in Phase 4.** Will filter arbitrage round-trips when the arb tagger ships in Phase 5.

parameters.name: parse_jupiter
parameters.description: **No-op in Phase 4.** Will expand Jupiter aggregator hops and attribute trades to source/destination tokens in Phase 5.

get.description:
Returns a daily portfolio-value series for the wallet. The chart is assembled on-demand with a cron-free model:

1. **Closed days** are read from the store and returned as-is — they are immutable once written.
2. **Gaps** (days between the last stored point and yesterday) are filled lazily: the FIFO engine replays the wallet's swaps to reconstruct end-of-day holdings, prices them against `ohlcv_1d` closes, and persists the result before returning.
3. **Today** is always computed live and returned as `today` with `provisional: true` — never persisted.

`value_usd` uses the **current** SOL/USD rate from the Pyth oracle for all points, including historical ones. Apply your own historical SOL/USD to `value_sol` if you need historically accurate USD values.

See [Wallet portfolio](/concepts/wallet-portfolio#cron-free-chart-freshness) for the full freshness model.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: How far back to go. `all` returns every day since the wallet's first indexed trade.

get.description:
Returns FIFO realized + unrealized PnL for the wallet over the specified window. Lot-based matching: buy lots pushed in time order, each sell matches against the earliest open lots and realizes `proceeds − cost_matched`.

The response always includes a `warning` field:

> `basic FIFO realized PnL; full cost-basis (cross-wallet, tax-lot, transfer-aware) ships in Phase 8`

This is intentional — FIFO is a good approximation for single-wallet trading but is not suitable for tax calculations or cross-wallet strategies. Full PnL ships in Phase 8.

Response cached 60 seconds.

See [Wallet portfolio](/concepts/wallet-portfolio#pnl-basic-fifo-realized-pnl).

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Time window to compute PnL over.

parameters.name: dex
parameters.description: Filter to a single DEX by integer ID. See `DexId` for the mapping.

get.description:
Returns the wallet's current native SOL balance via Shyft's `getBalance` JSON-RPC call, plus USD conversion via the Pyth oracle.

When Shyft is unavailable, `sol_balance` is `null` and the response still returns 200 — it never 500s on a Shyft blip. Response cached 30 seconds.

400.description: Wallet address is not valid base58

get.description:
Returns aggregate trading statistics for the wallet over the specified window: best and worst trades by realized PnL, win rate, total trade count, and total SOL volume.

An empty wallet (no indexed swaps) returns 200 with zero values. Response cached 60 seconds.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Time window to compute stats over.

parameters.name: dex
parameters.description: Filter to a single DEX by integer ID. See `DexId` for the mapping.

get.description:
Stream of mint-create / bonding-progress / graduation events for pump.fun tokens. Filter by lifecycle status to surface fresh mints (`new`), tokens close to the curve end (`completing`), tokens about to migrate (`graduating`), or completed migrations (`migrated`).

An unrecognized `status` value returns **HTTP 400** `{"error":"invalid_filter:status:<value>"}`.

For a real-time push of these events, see [WebSocket: pump lifecycle](/streaming/pump-lifecycle).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Lifecycle phase. `new` = recent mints; `completing` / `graduating` = bonding curve near 100%; `migrated` = already on pumpswap. Any other value → 400 `invalid_filter:status:<value>`.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Cursor — epoch seconds; returns events strictly older than this.

next_before_ts.description: Pass this back as `before` for the next page; null when exhausted.

get.description: Returns 200 if the API process is alive. Requires authentication like every other endpoint.
Returns 200 if dependencies (ClickHouse, cache) are healthy; 503 otherwise. Requires authentication.

Phase 8 cost-basis PnL summary for a wallet: realized + read-time-spot unrealized SOL, open/closed position counts, USD via Pyth, and a compact per-position list. Empty wallets return zeros and an empty `positions` array.

Cached 5s when any position traded in the last 5 minutes, otherwise 30s.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

get.description: Paginated cost-basis positions for a wallet. `min_value_sol` is applied **after** read-time pricing on `current_value_sol`. `total` is the true total under the LIMIT (`count() OVER ()`).

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

get.description: The single (wallet, mint) position, always glass-box (`realized_breakdown` + inline tags present). When no position exists, returns `200` with `position: null` (uncached).

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

position.description: Full position object (realized_breakdown always present), or `null`.

get.description: Compute-on-read daily PnL series. `from`/`to` are inclusive UTC `YYYY-MM-DD`. Sparse — one point per active day. Malformed dates return `400`. Cached 30s.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

get.description: Same compute-on-read daily series as `/history`, chart-shaped (`t`/`realized`/`unrealized`/`value`) plus a summary header whose `unrealized_sol` is read-time spot (reconciles with `/v2/pnl/wallets/{wallet}`). Cached 30s.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

get.description: Compute-on-read streak / drawdown / best-worst-day analytics. Headline drawdown is peak-to-trough of cumulative realized PnL; a secondary close-based equity-curve drawdown is also returned. Cached 5s hot / 30s cold.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

get.description: Standout positions for a wallet from one `wallet_positions` aggregate: biggest winner/loser, fastest flip, longest hold, most traded, biggest single buy/sell. Each highlight is `null` when no qualifying row matched. Cached 5s hot / 30s cold.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

get.description: Value-weighted concentration (top1/3/5 %, HHI) and exposure over PRICED open positions (read-time spot). Mode-independent (no `pnl_mode`). Concentration fields are `null` when total open value <= 0. Cached 5s hot / 30s cold.

concentration.description: Null fields when total open value <= 0.

get.description: Earliest buyers of a token, ordered by (first_buy_ts, first_slot, first_sig). With `enrich=1`, each buyer's current (wallet, mint) PnL is attached. Cached 60s.

parameters.name: enrich
parameters.description: When `1`, attaches each buyer's current (wallet, mint) PnL.

get.description: Traders of a token ranked by realized PnL (mode-selected, full breakdown included). `total` is the true total under LIMIT. `platform` and `dex` are accepted but ignored — each adds an entry to `warnings`. Cached 30s.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

post.description: Fetch many (wallet, mint) positions in one call. De-dups internally but aligns output to the **original** input order (duplicates echo the same object). `realized_breakdown` always present. Uncached. 1–200 pairs; empty → 400, >200 → 400.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

positions.description: Aligned to input order; `null` where no position exists.

post.summary: Batch positions for one wallet across mints
post.description: Positions for one wallet across many mints (`WHERE wallet=? AND mint IN(...)`). Aligned to the input `mints` (`null` for missing). `realized_breakdown` always present. 1–100 mints; empty → 400, >100 → 400.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

post.summary: Batch positions for one token across wallets
post.description: Positions for one token across many wallets (`WHERE mint=? AND wallet IN(...)`). Each row mirrors the `/traders` per-row object (wallet-keyed). Aligned to input `wallets` (`null` for missing). 1–200 wallets; empty → 400, >200 → 400.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

post.description: Per-wallet PnL summaries (`WHERE wallet IN(...)` grouped per wallet). Each `summaries` element is byte-identical to the single `GET /v2/pnl/wallets/{wallet}` body. Aligned to input `wallets` (`null` for a wallet with zero positions). 1–100 wallets; empty → 400, >100 → 400.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

get.description: Wallets ranked by realized PnL over a trailing window (wallets with `last_trade_ts` in the window). Served from an event-driven per-wallet rollup (`wallet_pnl_rollup`), cache-warmed for sub-second response (300s cache).

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

get.description: All-time KOL realized-PnL leaderboard (KOL set = DISTINCT wallet where tag=`kol`). Adds Twitter/display metadata. `exclude_arbitrage` defaults FALSE on KOL boards. Cached 300s.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

get.description: Same envelope as `/leaderboard/kols` plus a top-level `period`. Filters the KOL set to those active in the window, then ranks by ALL-TIME realized PnL. Cached 300s.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

get.description: Realized-only heatmap (rows = top-N KOLs, cols = days). Range defaults to the last 30 days, hard-capped at 90 days. `from > to` or a span > 90 days returns `400`. Cached 300s, single-flighted.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Inclusive end date (UTC, `YYYY-MM-DD`). Defaults to today UTC.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

days.description: Full inclusive [from, to] day column header.

get.summary: Top KOLs on a single day
get.description: Top KOLs ranked by realized PnL on one UTC day. `win_rate`/`trade_count` are intentionally omitted (not per-day derivable). Malformed date → `400`. Cached 300s.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: UTC day (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Defaults to yesterday UTC.

parameters.name: pnl_mode
parameters.description: Realized-PnL accounting mode. Unknown values fall back to `adjusted`.

get.description:
Flat query-string alias over `POST /screener` (`ScreenerFilters::from_query`). All params optional; ranges use min/max-prefixed names. Returns the identical **top-level** body to `POST /screener` — `{ results, total_matches, limit, offset, warnings }`, no `{success, data, error}` wrapper — and the same `x-cache` / `x-total-matches-exact` headers.

Inherits the screener error contract: a malformed `mint`/`creator` (non-base58) → **HTTP 400** `{"success":false,"data":null,"error":"invalid parameter: invalid_filter:mint|creator|mints"}`; an unsupported filter (e.g. `minRiskScore`/`maxRiskScore`) stays **200** with a `warnings[]` note.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Set `compact` for the compact row variant.

schema.description: Identical top-level body to POST /screener (no success/data/error wrapper).

400.description: Malformed address filter (`mint` or `creator` not valid base58). Same enveloped body as `POST /screener`. Unsupported filters (e.g. `minRiskScore`) stay 200 with a `warnings[]` note.

get.description: Global list of known centralized-exchange wallets from the `exchange_wallets` snapshot, ordered by wallet. `count` is the full distinct-wallet total (`count() OVER ()`), independent of paging.

Wallets the bot tagger flagged (high swap frequency / cross-mint breadth over a rolling 1h window), from the `bot_wallets` snapshot, ordered by `swaps_1h` desc then `wallet` asc. `count` is the full total (`count() OVER ()`).

Most-recent liquidity-rug / drain events across **all venues**, newest first, over a **7-day rolling window**. Reads the `rug_detected` rows the rug classifier persists. `drained_pct` is a **0..1 fraction** (not a percentage) and `lp_sol_pulled` is **already in SOL** (lamports ÷ 1e9). The live-only `reason`/`graduated` fields are **not** on this REST shape — use the [`rugs` WebSocket room](/streaming/websocket#the-global-rugs-room) for those. **Requires an API key** (header `X-API-Key`, or `?api_key=` / `Authorization: Bearer`).

parameters.name: min_drained_pct
parameters.description: Only return rugs with `drained_pct >= ` this **0..1** fraction (default `0` = all). Clamped 0..1.

pool_address.description: On-chain pool / LP account that was drained.

classification.description: `RUGGED` = a graduated/AMM pool drained past the rug threshold (>95%); `LIQUIDITY_DRAINING` = a large (50–95%) drain that isn't a full rug.

drained_pct.description: Fraction of pool liquidity removed, **0..1** (NOT a 0–100 percentage). e.g. `0.97` = 97%.

lp_sol_pulled.description: SOL liquidity removed, **already in SOL** (the handler divides the raw `lamports` by `1e9` for you — do **not** divide again).

signature.description: Base58 signature of the triggering withdraw/burn.

count.description: Full match total (`count() OVER ()`), independent of `limit`/`offset`.

get.description: Rug / liquidity-drain history for a single mint, newest first (no 7-day bound — uses the `token_mint` sort key). Same row shape as `GET /rugs`: `drained_pct` is a **0..1 fraction**, `lp_sol_pulled` is **already in SOL**. The live-only `reason`/`graduated` fields are WS-only. **Requires an API key.**

parameters.name: mint
parameters.description: Token mint (base58). A malformed mint returns **400**.

mint.description: The mint echoed back from the path.

pool_address.description: On-chain pool / LP account that was drained.

classification.description: `RUGGED` = a graduated/AMM pool drained past the rug threshold (>95%); `LIQUIDITY_DRAINING` = a large (50–95%) drain that isn't a full rug.

drained_pct.description: Fraction of pool liquidity removed, **0..1** (NOT a 0–100 percentage). e.g. `0.97` = 97%.

lp_sol_pulled.description: SOL liquidity removed, **already in SOL** (the handler divides the raw `lamports` by `1e9` for you — do **not** divide again).

signature.description: Base58 signature of the triggering withdraw/burn.

count.description: Full match total (`count() OVER ()`), independent of `limit`/`offset`.
